title: Substack vs Sendinblue (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Substack vs Sendinblue. Learn which email marketing platform suits your needs best.
h1: Substack vs Sendinblue: A Detailed Comparison
intro: Substack and Sendinblue (now Brevo) serve different audiences within the email marketing space. Substack is built for independent writers and creators seeking direct monetization from subscribers, while Sendinblue is an all-in-one CRM platform designed for transactional emails, SMS, and business automation. Choosing between them depends on your primary goal: newsletter publishing with revenue sharing or comprehensive marketing automation.

comparison_table.tool_b: Freemium with paid plans starting at $25/month
Up to 100,000 contacts with 300 free emails/day

comparison_table.tool_a: Paid newsletter subscriptions, podcast hosting, built-in audience discovery
comparison_table.tool_b: Transactional email, SMS, WhatsApp, CRM, chat functionality
Businesses needing omnichannel marketing and CRM integration

choose_a_if: You're a writer or creator looking to monetize newsletters through paid subscriptions
You want unlimited subscriber access without per-contact fees
You prefer a platform with built-in audience discovery and community features
choose_b_if: You need transactional email and SMS capabilities for e-commerce or SaaS
You want integrated CRM, chat, and WhatsApp messaging in one platform
You're building a comprehensive marketing automation system for your business

faq.a: It depends on your use case. Substack excels for individual creators monetizing content, while Sendinblue is superior for businesses needing comprehensive email, SMS, and CRM features. Sendinblue has better overall ratings (4.5 G2, 4.3 Trustpilot vs. 4.0 and 2.9 respectively), suggesting broader satisfaction among business users.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Substack or Sendinblue?
faq.a: Substack is free forever—you only pay when generating revenue (10% commission on paid subscriptions). Sendinblue is also free for up to 100,000 contacts with 300 daily emails, but paid plans start at $25/month. For high-volume senders or businesses, Sendinblue's transparent pricing may be more predictable than Substack's revenue-share model.

faq.q: Can I switch from Sendinblue to Substack?
faq.a: Yes, you can export your contact list from Sendinblue and import it to Substack. However, the platforms serve different purposes—Sendinblue is for marketing automation while Substack is for newsletter publishing, so a direct migration only makes sense if changing business models entirely.
